name.es: Pan de masa madre con papilla de avena
name.fr: Pain au levain au porridge d'avoine

description.en: A soft, cooked oat porridge folded through the dough pushes effective hydration far past a normal loaf, for an exceptionally moist, long-keeping crumb with a mild, toasty sweetness. Sticky and unnerving to handle, but trust the process.
description.de: Ein weicher, gekochter Haferporridge, untergearbeitet in den Teig, treibt die effektive Hydration weit über ein normales Brot hinaus – für eine außergewöhnlich saftige, lange haltbare Krume mit milder, gerösteter Süße. Klebrig in der Handhabung, aber es lohnt sich zu vertrauen.
description.es: Una suave papilla de avena cocida, incorporada a la masa, empuja la hidratación efectiva muy por encima de un pan normal, dando una miga excepcionalmente húmeda y de larga conservación con un dulzor suave y tostado. Pegajosa de manejar, pero confía en el proceso.
description.fr: Un porridge d'avoine cuit et incorporé à la pâte pousse l'hydratation effective bien au-delà d'un pain classique, pour une mie exceptionnellement moelleuse et longue conservation, avec une douceur toastée subtile. Collante à travailler, mais il faut faire confiance au processus.

origin.note: Cooked-grain porridge breads have roots across Northern Europe; this specific high-hydration sourdough treatment is a contemporary artisan formula.

ingredients.note: cooked into a porridge, plus extra for rolling the shaped loaf

keeping.note: The cooked porridge keeps the crumb moist noticeably longer than a plain sourdough.

summary.en: Mix starter with flour and water; ripen about 5-6 hours.
Simmer rolled oats in water, covered, about 16 minutes to a creamy porridge; spread on a tray and cool completely.
Dissolve the levain in water, add both flours, and rest 1 hour; then work in salt.
Fold the cooled porridge into the dough during the first stretch-and-fold, then continue with six sets total over about 5.5 hours, pinching apart any oat clumps.
Divide, preshape, rest 20 minutes, then shape into boules and coat the bottoms in dry rolled oats.
Proof 1 hour at room temperature, then overnight in the refrigerator.
Bake in a preheated Dutch oven at 230°C: 20 minutes covered with steam, then about 35 minutes uncovered until deep golden.
Rest at least 1-2 hours before cutting; the interior needs time to fully set.

source.author: Maurizio Leo
source.adaptation: Hydration counts only part of the porridge's cooking water as effective dough hydration, since much of it is bound into the cooked starch rather than behaving as free water.
